failedAssertions.reason: Registration still does not redirect to onboarding; it remains on /register with a success card.

After the clean restart, the stale runtime crash is gone, but the RBAC outcome is still incorrect: non-owner billing access redirects to an org-scoped permission-denied URL that 404s instead of showing a real Access Denied/403 experience.
The restricted action path still fails at the routing surface with 405 instead of returning a 403 permission denial for the viewer role.
The promotion/JWT-refresh half works, but the required pre-promotion unauthorized redirect does not happen and the promoted admin still cannot load the billing surface because that route 404s.

The empty-name validation now works after restart, but the over-maximum-length requirement still fails: the control silently truncates input and lets the user proceed instead of showing a visible max-length validation message.
The owner-facing team list does not show a revoked status; the invite simply disappears, and logged-out reuse of the link redirects to login instead of directly surfacing the revoked error.
Required contact-email and address fields are still missing, and the updated organisation name did not reflect immediately across the dashboard header after save.
The second organisation is created and added to the switcher, but the real user flow still ends on a broken /api/orgs/switch 404 instead of a clean post-create destination.
Metadata fields are populated, but canonical/share URLs still use localhost instead of a public-safe origin, so the assertion does not fully pass.

The clean restart removed the earlier stale-runtime blocker and the real first-visit flow now reaches dashboard successfully, but the assertion still fails because the final dashboard is generic rather than domain-appropriate after choosing Children's Residential Homes. Free-plan state, owner role, and activeOrgId are observable; domain-personalised widgets are not.
The central audit page lacks the required user filter, and no entity-level audit tab was available from the implemented foundation routes.

blockedAssertions.blockedBy: Deferred to m2-person-dashboard: Google OAuth validation requires real provider credentials that are not available for the foundation milestone environment.
Deferred to m2-person-dashboard: Google OAuth account-linking validation requires real provider credentials that are not available for the foundation milestone environment.
Deferred to m2-person-dashboard: domain-adaptive terminology and dashboard behavior depend on milestone-2 person/dashboard features.
Deferred to m2-person-dashboard: combined domain tooling on the dashboard depends on milestone-2 person/dashboard features.
Deferred to m2-person-dashboard: the required domain-specific dashboard tools are implemented in the later person/dashboard milestone, not foundation.

The central audit page now works for organisation create/update entries, but the real UI still lacks accessible care-significant create/update/delete surfaces, so the assertion cannot be fully verified against persons, care plans, or daily notes.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that stale Next.js runtime states can break auth/onboarding/billing validation and that a clean PORT=3200 bun run dev restart should be attempted before classifying those symptoms as product failures.

orchestratorOverride.reason: After 3 validation rounds and 4 fix features, 48/66 foundation assertions pass. 12 remaining failures moved to misc-foundation-fixes (misc-1 milestone). 5 domain-adaptation assertions deferred to m2-person-dashboard. Core infrastructure (auth, RBAC, multi-tenancy, data isolation, audit trail, notifications, marketing) is functional. Remaining issues are UI polish and routing edge cases that will be naturally resolved as more features are built.
